records.ety: [F. continuer, L. continuare, -tinuatum, to connect, continue, fr. continuus. See Continuous, and cf. Continuate.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Continued (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Continuing.]

defs: To remain in a given place or condition; to remain in connection with; to abide; to stay.
quotes: Here to continue, and build up here A growing empire.
They continue with me now three days, and have nothing to eat.

defs: To be permanent or durable; to endure; to last.
quotes: But now thy kingdom shall not continue.

defs: To be steadfast or constant in any course; to persevere; to abide; to endure; to persist; to keep up or maintain a particular condition, course, or series of actions; as, the army continued to advance.
quotes: If ye continue in my word, then are ye my disciples indeed.
the use of the navel is to continue the infant unto the mother.

defs: To protract or extend in duration; to preserve or persist in; to cease not.
quotes: O continue thy loving kindness unto them that know thee.
You know how to make yourself happy by only continuing such a life as you have been long accustomed to lead.

defs: To carry onward or extend; to prolong or produce; to add to or draw out in length.
quotes: A bridge of wond'rous length, From hell continued, reaching th' utmost orb of this frail world.

defs: To retain; to suffer or cause to remain; as, the trustees were continued; also, to suffer to live.
quotes: And how shall we continue Claudio.
